results.title: HarnessBridge: A Learnable Bidirectional Controller for LLM Agents
results.content:
Introducing 'HarnessBridge', an agent-environmental interface module, to solve the problem of duplicate information accumulation and inefficient interaction that occurs during long-term work. HarnessBridge optimizes the flow of information using learnable bidirectional projection technology between large-scale language model (LLM) agents and environments. Specifically, observation projection reduces token usage by compressing the massive execution history into a core state, and action projection increases work efficiency by blocking unfounded suggestions and providing feedback. According to the results of the study, the system has shown excellent versatility while dramatically reducing token consumption compared to conventional manual design, maintaining or improving performance in a [...] manual design, maintaining or improving performance in a variety of models and environments. In conclusion, this paper presents a new paradigm that optimizes the agent interface through learned policies instead of fixed rules. [...] # HarnessBridge: A Learnable Bidirectional Controller for LLM Agents
## Research Paper Review

results.title: Can you add a bridge to a ropes access harness? : r/TreeClimbing
results.content:
# Can you add a bridge to a ropes access harness? : r/TreeClimbing
Skip to main contentCan you add a bridge to a ropes access harness? : r/TreeClimbing

Open menu Open navigation.

I recommend considering a Petzl Sequoia SRT with a Petzl TOP chest harness - it has pretty much all the features as your rope access harness, plus additional ones specifically for tree work, and it's far more adjustable and versatile than your harness.

But if you're not cutting down trees yet and are only climbing them... I think your harness will be fine. Don't bother adding a bridge to it. [...] You're supposed to have two harnesses anyway - one for you, and one for whoever is going to climb up and rescue you if something goes wrong. Your ground crew might not know enough to cut down a tree while climbing it, but at least one of them really should know enough to climb a tree safely and they can use your rope access harness for that. Calling 911 will take far too long for most emergencies.

, bc it's rated for fall arrest unlike the suspenders on my monkey beaver and was about the same price as getting a fall arrest unit for my Buckingham ergovation. [...] Continue with Phone Number

## AI for DevOps & Automation

Unleash developer productivity with AI that understands your DevOps ecosystem. Harness combines the industry's fastest, most secure CI/CD with developer self-service to automate pipelines, infrastructure, and the entire path from code to production.

### Continuous Delivery & GitOps [...] ## Hear from our customers

See why hundreds of DevOps and engineering teams trust Harness to automate processes, reduce manual interventions, and consolidates tools.

### Citi improves software delivery performance with Harness

Harness CD lets us release each change within minutes of a pull request being merged. Running all the necessary tests and scans during the pipeline gives us the confidence to do this.

Stefanos Piperoglou  
Technical Program Manager, Citi Bank

### United Airlines Accelerates Deployments by 75% With Harness

By choosing Harness for CI and CD, we were able to give the governance policies to the developers and create the guardrails we needed. Harness gives us a platform rather than just a DevOps tool.
